# `sesam uninstall` must remove every trace of sesam's git integration: the
# local git config entries (alias + diff driver, checked here as they install by
# default) and the lines sesam wrote to .gitattributes / .gitignore. Without
# --all it leaves the repository data (.sesam/, sesam.yml) in place; with --all
# it removes those too.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin

# Wiring is present after init.
exec git config --local alias.sesam
stdout '^!sesam$'
exec git config --local diff.sesam-diff.textconv
stdout '^sesam show$'
exists .gitattributes
grep 'sesam-diff' .gitattributes
exists .gitignore
grep '.sesam' .gitignore

# Uninstall without --all strips the git integration but keeps the data.
exec sesam uninstall

# The bare alias option is a regression guard: git aliases cannot live in a
# subsection, so removing them needs RemoveOption, not RemoveSubsection.
! exec git config --local alias.sesam
! exec git config --local diff.sesam-diff.textconv
# Both managed files held only sesam's lines, so they are gone entirely.
! exists .gitattributes
! exists .gitignore
# Data is untouched without --all.
exists .sesam
exists sesam.yml

# --all also removes the repository data (--no-ask skips the confirmation).
exec sesam uninstall --all --no-ask
! exists .sesam
! exists sesam.yml
